manufacturer.comment: Sold under the Orange Pi brand by Shenzhen Xunlong Software Co., Ltd.

release-date.comment: Announced 2023-05-10 by CNX-Software; broad sales began the week of 2023-05-13. The 32 GB RAM variant followed in October 2023.

status.comment: Verified 2026-04-26: official product page still active, Armbian publishes current 6.12 builds, retailers in stock.

open-source-hardware.comment: Orange Pi publishes a schematic PDF in the downloads section but no PCB layout source / Gerber / BOM. Per RESEARCH.md, schematics-only does not qualify as OSHW.

country-of-origin.comment: Shenzhen Xunlong Software is headquartered in Shenzhen, Guangdong.

soc.comment: Full RK3588 (not the cut-down RK3588S used on the standard Orange Pi 5).

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: A76 cluster up to 2.4 GHz, A55 cluster up to 1.8 GHz.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G610 supports OpenGL ES 1.1/2.0/3.2, OpenCL 2.2, Vulkan 1.2.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G610 supports Vulkan 1.2 (Arm Bifrost/Valhall driver and mainline Panthor).

npu-performance-tops.comment: Vendor-quoted 6 TOPS @ INT8 (RK3588 tri-core NPU); supports INT4/INT8/INT16/FP16.

hw-video-decode.comment: RK3588 decodes 8K@60 H.265/H.264/VP9/AVS2 and 8K@60 AV1; identical to other RK3588 boards.

hw-video-encode.comment: Up to 8K@30 encode for H.264/H.265. AV1 encode is NOT supported on RK3588.

ram-max-gb.comment: Originally 4/8/16 GB at launch (May 2023); 32 GB LPDDR4X SKU added October 2023.

ram-type.comment: Vendor lists 'LPDDR4/4x' for the 4/8/16 GB SKUs (mix depending on production batch); the 32 GB SKU is explicitly LPDDR4X.

emmc.comment: eMMC socket (not soldered); supports 16/32/64/128/256 GB Orange Pi modules.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 2280 M-key, PCIe 3.0 x4, vendor-rated up to 2,000 MB/s.

sata.comment: No native SATA port; storage attaches via the M.2 NVMe slot or USB.

ethernet.comment: Two 2.5GbE RJ45 ports via Realtek RTL8125BG controllers, each on its own PCIe lane.

dual-ethernet.comment: Two RJ45 ports, both 2.5 GbE — distinguishing feature versus the standard Orange Pi 5 / Rock 5B.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi. Optional Orange Pi Wi-Fi 6E + BT 5.2 module attaches via the M.2 E-key slot.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; only available when the optional M.2 E-key wireless module is fitted (BT 5.2).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Board has 2× USB-C: one is power-only (5V/4A in), the other is USB 3.1 Gen 1 data with DisplayPort 1.4 alt-mode. Only the data port is counted here.

poe-support.comment: No PoE pads on the Ethernet ports and no documented PoE HAT path; power is via the dedicated USB-C input.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 2× HDMI 2.1 outputs, each up to 8K@60. There is also a separate HDMI 2.0 INPUT (4K@60 capture) — not counted here.

displayport.comment: DisplayPort 1.4 via the USB-C data port (alt-mode); no dedicated DP connector.

mipi-dsi.comment: 2× 4-lane MIPI DSI connectors, each up to 4K@60, plus a touch-panel FPC socket.

audio-jack.comment: 3.5 mm jack via ES8388 codec; onboard MIC and speaker connector also present.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin header in Raspberry Pi-style pinout (UART, I2C, SPI, CAN, I2S, SDIO, PWM, GPIO).

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pinout is RPi-compatible, but the board is 100×75 mm with non-standard mounting holes and no HAT EEPROM, so most HATs will not mount mechanically or auto-configure.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: M.2 M-key 2280 for NVMe SSD; M.2 E-key for the Wi-Fi 6 / BT module. Two further PCIe lanes are consumed internally by the dual RTL8125BG 2.5GbE PHYs.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I2C, SPI, UART, plus CAN, I2S, SDIO, PWM all available on the 40-pin header.

power-input.comment: Dedicated USB-C power input, 5V/4A (not USB-PD); RK806-1 PMU on board. Can also accept 5V via the GPIO header.

idle-power-w.comment: Reviewer measurement: 2–3 W idle at 5V (no NVMe / no Wi-Fi module). Bret.dk and other reviews report up to ~3.5 W idle once an NVMe SSD is fitted.

load-power-w.comment: Reviewer measurement: 6–8 W under full load at 5V (CPU stress, no extra peripherals). Total board draw scales higher (10–15 W) once an NVMe SSD and Wi-Fi module are added.

cooling-required.comment: RK3588 throttles under sustained load on a passive heatsink alone; reviewers used a fan-on-heatsink setup to prevent throttling. Orange Pi sells an official 40 mm fan + heatsink kit.

width-mm.comment: Vendor data sheet quotes 100×75 mm; some retailer listings round to 100×70 mm. CNX-Software (citing Orange Pi spec sheet) uses 100×75.

form-factor-class.comment: 100×75 mm — close to but not quite Pico-ITX (100×72). Per RESEARCH.md threshold this is custom, not Pi-standard.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Orange Pi-specific hole pattern; not RPi standard.

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight quoted in vendor spec sheet.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Armbian publishes both 'current' (mainline 6.12) and 'vendor' (6.1) tracks. Mainline boots and runs CPU/USB3/PCIe/2.5GbE/Mali-G610, but several RK3588 features (full HDMI 2.1, NPU, hardware video codecs) still need the Rockchip BSP kernel for 100% functionality. Verified 2026-04-26.

official-os-image.comment: Orange Pi publishes Ubuntu 22.04, Debian 11, Orange Pi OS (Arch / Droid), and Android 12 images.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor publishes Ubuntu and Debian; Armbian provides 15 board-specific images (current + vendor kernels); DietPi lists Orange Pi 5 Plus among supported boards. Joshua-Riek's ubuntu-rockchip project also provides well-regarded images. Manjaro/Arch ARM/Kali also exist but Kali ships under Armbian's umbrella here.

bootloader.comment: U-Boot via Rockchip SPL loader; Armbian's recent builds use mainline ATF and U-Boot v2026.01.

long-term-support.comment: Orange Pi does not publish a formal support window. Practical support comes from the broader RK3588 mainlining effort (Collabora, Armbian) plus vendor BSP updates; multi-year coverage seems likely but is not contractually guaranteed.

msrp-base-usd.comment: 4 GB launch MSRP (May 2023). 2026 retail typically $99–$120 for the base SKU at major resellers.

msrp-top-usd.comment: 32 GB SKU launch MSRP (October 2023). The earlier 16 GB top tier launched at $129; 32 GB became the new top in October 2023.

available-regions.comment: Sold via Orange Pi's distributor network (orangepi.net for NA/EU), AliExpress (APAC/global), and Amazon. Verified 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: all RAM tiers (4/8/16/32 GB) currently listed in stock at multiple distributors.

documentation-quality.comment: User manual, schematic PDF, datasheet, and OS images are published, but the wiki is dated, organisation is awkward, and English translations are uneven. Adequate for getting started but well below RPi or Radxa-tier.

community-size.comment: Orange Pi sits in the large tier per RESEARCH.md thresholds — sizeable Armbian sub-forum, active r/OrangePI, broad Joshua-Riek/ubuntu-rockchip user base, and a dedicated CNX-Software / LinuxGizmos cadence.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum.orangepi.org (English subforum) plus active third-party communities on Armbian, Reddit, and the Joshua-Riek Discord.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Official metal case, 5V/4A PSU, 40 mm fan + heatsink, Wi-Fi 6E module, eMMC modules, and MIPI camera options. Decent first-party set; broader 40-pin HAT compatibility helps but mechanical fit is limited.
